title: Why this chronicle matters now
summary: The point of the page is not to memorialize an old enterprise-security run. It is to argue that Sean Xiang's long build becomes newly legible in the AI-infrastructure era, where data-at-rest protection, cryptographic trust, and accelerator-era architecture have to meet again.

signals.label: Long-cycle credibility
signals.text: The Bloombase record shows repeated interoperability and alliance milestones across more than one infrastructure generation, which is a stronger founder signal than a short burst of hype.

signals.label: AI-era translation
signals.text: The 2023 NVIDIA framing and 2025 Utimaco alliance together suggest an active attempt to reinterpret prior security primitives for GPU, DPU, and AI-data environments.

signals.label: Editorial gap this site fills
signals.text: Public traces exist, but they are scattered across company news pages, third-party profiles, and event visibility. The chronicle turns that dispersed evidence into a coherent, source-backed thesis.

nextMoves: Capture stronger direct sources for the earliest formation chapter so the opening story can move from restrained inference to firmer evidence.
Add real media only where the archive supports it: conference visuals, article snippets, or document scans that deepen trust instead of decorating the page.
Keep the site dual-mode: skim-friendly for busy visitors, but strong enough that an investor, operator, or researcher can audit the record.
